citations.narrative:
The interior sides of the outdoor housing and shelter structure used to house one Ring-Tailed lemur had areas of
unsealed wooden boards present. In addition, the lemur had access to a smaller metal cage, where surfaces throughout
its frame has began to rust. The facility representative informed the inspector they’d noticed these surfaces as well, with
plans to repair the metal cage soon.
Unsealed surfaces are difficult to effectively clean and sanitize, and do not prevent the absorption of liquids and moisture.
Surfaces that nonhuman primates come into contact with must be able to be readily cleaned and sanitized, or replaced
when worn or soiled.
Correct by March 9, 2026.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the facility representative and licensee.
n

narrative:
3.80(a)(2)(ix) 
   
Primary enclosures. 
The interior sides of the outdoor housing and shelter structure used to house one Ring-Tailed lemur had areas of 
unsealed wooden boards present. In addition, the lemur had access to a smaller metal cage, where surfaces throughout 
its frame has began to rust. The facility representative informed the inspector they’d noticed these surfaces as well, with 
plans to repair the metal cage soon.  
Unsealed surfaces are difficult to effectively clean and sanitize, and do not prevent the absorption of liquids and moisture. 
Surfaces that nonhuman primates come into contact with must be able to be readily cleaned and sanitized, or replaced 
when worn or soiled.  
 
Correct by March 9, 2026. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the facility representative and licensee.
